ddls:
CREATE TABLE "job_postings" (
  "posting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job posting */,
  "date_posted" TEXT /* Date the job was posted */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Title of the job */,
  "employer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employer */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source */,
  "job_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job type */,
  "salary_min" TEXT /* Minimum salary for the job */,
  "salary_max" TEXT /* Maximum salary for the job */,
  "job_description" TEXT /* Description of the job */,
  "application_deadline" TEXT /* Deadline for job applications */,
  "number_of_openings" INTEGER /* Number of openings for the job */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("posting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_job_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_type_id") REFERENCES job_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_employer_id FOREIGN KEY ("employer_id") REFERENCES employers ("employer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employers" (
  "employer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employer */,
  "employer_name" TEXT /* Name of the employer */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry the employer operates in */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the employer */,
  "website" TEXT /* Website of the employer */,
  "headquarters_location" TEXT /* Headquarters location of the employer */,
  "number_of_employees" INTEGER /* Number of employees in the company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "province" TEXT /* Province the location is in */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country the location is in */,
  "postal_code" TEXT /* Postal code of the location */,
  "latitude" TEXT /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" TEXT /* Longitude of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of source (e.g., job board, company website) */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the job type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the job type */,
  "typical_work_hours" TEXT /* Typical work hours for the job type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_industries" (
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each industry */,
  "industry_name" TEXT /* Name of the industry */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the industry */,
  "average_salary" TEXT /* Average salary in the industry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("industry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "skills" (
  "skill_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each skill */,
  "skill_name" TEXT /* Name of the skill */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the skill */,
  "skill_level" TEXT /* Skill level (e.g., beginner, intermediate, advanced) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("skill_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_posting_skills" (
  "posting_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job posting */,
  "skill_id" INTEGER /* ID of the skill */,
  "proficiency_required" TEXT /* Proficiency level required for the skill */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("posting_id", "skill_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "team" TEXT /* Team of the user */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date the user last logged in */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each job posting
Number of openings for the job
Number of employees in the company
Type of source (e.g., job board, company website)
Unique identifier for each job type
Typical work hours for the job type
Proficiency level required for the skill
Role of the user (e.g., researcher, analyst, admin)
Date the user last logged in
Status of the user (e.g., active, inactive)
